---
name: Critique / counterexample
about: Challenge a claim, definition, invariant, or model. This project wants to be falsified — a concrete counterexample is the most useful thing you can send.
title: "[critique] "
labels: critique
---

<!--
Thank you for trying to break this. A candidate that survives a real attempt is
worth more than one no one challenged. Be as concrete as you can; a single
constructed counterexample or countermodel is enough to open the conversation.
See METHOD.md for evidence labels and CONTRIBUTING.md for how changes are handled.
-->

## Which artifact and version

<!-- e.g. MEMORY_TAXONOMY v0.1, term `LCMRP-TAX-ROL-002@0.1`; or FMO-0.1 invariant
`FMO-INV-14`; or hypothesis H1. Name the exact version/ID so the challenge binds
to specific bytes. -->

## The specific claim you are challenging

<!-- Quote or paraphrase the exact commitment: a necessary/sufficient condition,
an invariant, a non-entailment, a crosswalk row, an axis-independence claim, etc. -->

## Counterexample or countermodel

<!-- The constructed case, structure, or argument that the claim cannot handle.
Include only the facts needed to make the point. If it maps to an existing case
in docs/taxonomy/cases/, say which. -->

## Proposed change (optional)

<!-- What should happen if the challenge holds: narrow a definition, drop/merge an
axis, supersede a term version, add a case, reject an organization, etc. It's fine
to leave this blank and just report the problem. -->
